responses.What year did World War II end?: world war ii ended in 1945, with germany surrendering in may and japan surrendering in september following the atomic bombings of hiroshima and nagasaki.
responses.How many bones are in the human body?: an adult human body typically has 206 bones. this number can vary slightly depending on individual factors, and newborns have more bones that fuse together as they grow.
responses.What is the hardest natural substance?: diamond is considered the hardest natural substance on earth. it ranks 10 on the mohs scale of mineral hardness, which means it can scratch all other natural materials.
responses.What is the chemical formula for water?: the chemical formula for water is h2o, which means each water molecule consists of two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.
responses.How many planets are in our solar system: there are 8 planets in our solar system: mercury, venus, earth, mars, jupiter, saturn, uranus, and neptune. pluto was previously considered the 9th planet but was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006.
responses.What is the capital of France?: paris is the capital of france. it is located in the north-central part of the country and is the largest city in france.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for gold?: the chemical symbol for gold is au, which comes from the latin word "aurum" meaning gold. it is a precious metal with the atomic number 79 on the periodic table.
responses.What is the largest planet in our solar: jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system, with a diameter of approximately 86,881 miles (139,820 kilometers). it is a gas giant and more than twice as massive as all the other planets combined.
responses.How many continents are there?: there are 7 continents: north america, south america, europe, asia, africa, australia (or oceania), and antarctica. some geographers and educational systems may have slight variations in how they define and count continents.
responses.What is the longest river in the world?: the nile river in africa is considered the longest river in the world, stretching approximately 6,650 kilometers (4,132 miles) from its source in burundi to its mouth in egypt where it empties into the mediterranean sea.
responses.What is the tallest mountain?: mount everest, located in the himalayas on the border between nepal and tibet, is the tallest mountain in the world, with a peak elevation of 29,031.7 feet (8,848.86 meters) above sea level.
responses.Who wrote Romeo and Juliet?: william shakespeare wrote the famous tragic play romeo and juliet, which was likely written between 1591 and 1595. it is one of shakespeare's most well-known works and tells the story of two young star-crossed lovers from feuding families in verona, italy.
responses.How many days are in a leap year?: a leap year has 366 days, with february containing 29 days instead of the usual 28. leap years occur every 4 years, with some exceptions, to keep the calendar year synchronized with the astronomical year.
responses.What is the speed of light?: the speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 meters per second (or about 186,282 miles per second). this is considered a fundamental constant of nature and is often denoted by the symbol "c" in scientific equations.
responses.Which ocean is the largest?: the pacific ocean is the largest ocean in the world, covering approximately 63 million square miles and representing about 46% of the earth's water surface. it is larger than all of earth's land area combined and stretches from the arctic ocean in the north to the southern ocean in the south.
responses.Who painted the Mona Lisa?: leonardo da vinci painted the mona lisa, a famous portrait created between 1503-1519. the painting is now housed in the louvre museum in paris, france.
responses.What is photosynthesis?: photosynthesis is a process used by plants and other organisms to convert light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose or other sugars. during this process, plants take in carbon dioxide and water, use sunlight to transform these substances, and release oxygen as a byproduct.
responses.Who invented the telephone?: alexander graham bell is credited with inventing the first practical telephone in 1876, though there were other inventors working on similar technologies at the time. bell was granted a patent for his telephone design and made the first successful telephone call on march 10, 1876.
responses.Who discovered penicillin?: alexander fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 when he noticed that a mold called penicillium notatum had contaminated one of his bacterial culture plates and was preventing bacterial growth. however, it was ernst chain and howard florey who later developed penicillin into a usable medical treatment during world war ii.
responses.What gas do plants absorb from the atmos: plants absorb carbon dioxide (co2) from the atmosphere during photosynthesis. they use this gas to produce oxygen and glucose, which helps them grow and provides oxygen for other organisms.
responses.What is the freezing point of water in F: the freezing point of water is 32 degrees fahrenheit (0 degrees celsius). at this temperature, water changes from a liquid state to a solid state (ice).
responses.What is the atomic number of carbon?: the atomic number of carbon is 6. this means that a carbon atom has 6 protons in its nucleus.
responses.Who was the first person to walk on the: neil armstrong was the first person to walk on the moon on july 20, 1969, during the apollo 11 mission. he famously said "that's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind" as he stepped onto the lunar surface.
responses.What element does the symbol 'Fe' repres: fe is the chemical symbol for iron, a metallic element with atomic number 26. iron is a common and important metal used in construction, manufacturing, and many other industries.
responses.What is the square root of 144?: the square root of 144 is 12. this is because 12 multiplied by itself equals 144.
responses.What is the largest desert in the world?: the antarctic desert is the largest desert in the world, covering approximately 5.5 million square miles. although most people think of hot, sandy deserts, antarctica qualifies as a desert due to its extremely low precipitation.
responses.Who wrote '1984'?: george orwell (born eric arthur blair) wrote the dystopian novel "1984", which was published in 1949. it is a famous work of fiction that explores themes of totalitarianism, government surveillance, and the manipulation of truth.
responses.What is the smallest country in the worl: vatican city, an independent city-state located within rome, italy, is the smallest country in the world, with an area of approximately 0.44 square kilometers (0.17 square miles). it is the headquarters of the roman catholic church and home to the pope.
responses.How many teeth does an adult human have?: an adult human typically has 32 teeth, including 8 incisors, 4 canines, 8 premolars, and 12 molars (including 4 wisdom teeth). however, some people have their wisdom teeth removed, which can reduce the total number to 28 teeth.
responses.How many sides does a hexagon have?: a hexagon has 6 sides. it is a polygon with six straight sides and six angles.
responses.What is the most abundant gas in Earth's: nitrogen is the most abundant gas in earth's atmosphere, making up approximately 78% of the air. the remaining 21% is primarily oxygen, with trace amounts of other gases like argon and carbon dioxide.
responses.Who developed the theory of relativity?: albert einstein developed the theory of relativity, with his special theory of relativity published in 1905 and his general theory of relativity completed in 1915. these groundbreaking theories fundamentally changed our understanding of space, time, gravity, and the relationship between energy and matter.
responses.What is the currency of the United Kingd: the currency of the united kingdom is the pound sterling (â£), often simply called the british pound. it is symbolized by the â£ sign and is subdivided into 100 pence.
responses.How many chambers does the human heart h: the human heart has 4 chambers: two atria (upper chambers) and two ventricles (lower chambers). the right side pumps deoxygenated blood to the lungs, while the left side pumps oxygenated blood to the rest of the body.
responses.What year was the Declaration of Indepen: the declaration of independence was primarily signed on august 2, 1776, though the continental congress had approved the document on july 4, 1776, which is now celebrated as independence day in the united states.
responses.What is the boiling point of water?: the boiling point of water is 100 degrees celsius (212 degrees fahrenheit) at standard atmospheric pressure (sea level). at higher altitudes, water boils at a lower temperature due to decreased atmospheric pressure.
responses.Who composed the Four Seasons?: antonio vivaldi composed the four seasons (le quattro stagioni), a set of four violin concertos that were published in 1725 as part of a larger work called il cimento dell'armonia e dell'inventione (the contest between harmony and invention).
responses.How many strings does a standard guitar: a standard guitar has 6 strings. these strings are typically tuned to e, a, d, g, b, and e from the lowest (thickest) to the highest (thinnest) string.
responses.Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?: jane austen wrote "pride and prejudice," which was first published in 1813. it is one of her most famous novels and is considered a classic of english literature.
responses.How many chromosomes do humans have?: humans have 46 chromosomes in total, arranged in 23 pairs. these chromosomes contain the genetic information that determines our traits and characteristics.
responses.What language has the most native speake: mandarin chinese has the most native speakers, with approximately 918 million people speaking it as their first language, primarily in china and taiwan.
responses.What is the distance from the Earth to t: the average distance from the earth to the sun is approximately 93 million miles (149.6 million kilometers), which is also known as one astronomical unit (au). this distance varies slightly throughout the year due to the earth's elliptical orbit.
responses.What is the largest organ in the human b: the skin is the largest organ in the human body. it covers the entire external surface of the body and plays crucial roles in protection, temperature regulation, and sensory perception.
responses.What is the capital of Australia?: canberra is the capital city of australia, located in the australian capital territory. it was purpose-built as the national capital and is situated between the country's two largest cities, sydney and melbourne.
responses.What is the capital of Japan?: tokyo is the capital of japan. it is the largest city in the country and serves as its political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.How many Great Lakes are there?: there are 5 great lakes: superior, michigan, huron, erie, and ontario. these large freshwater lakes are located in north america, primarily between the united states and canada.
responses.What is the speed of sound in air?: the speed of sound in air at sea level and at 20â°c (68â°f) is approximately 343 meters per second (1,235 kilometers per hour or 767 miles per hour). this speed can vary slightly depending on factors like temperature, humidity, and altitude.
responses.What is the pH of pure water?: the ph of pure water at room temperature (25â°c) is 7.0, which is considered neutral on the ph scale. this means pure water has an equal concentration of hydrogen (h+) and hydroxide (oh-) ions.
responses.What is the deepest ocean trench?: the mariana trench in the pacific ocean is the deepest known ocean trench, reaching a maximum depth of approximately 36,070 feet (10,994 meters) at a location called the challenger deep.
responses.How many vertices does a cube have?: a cube has 8 vertices. these vertices are the points where three edges of the cube meet at each corner.
responses.What is the capital of Canada?: ottawa is the capital city of canada, located in the province of ontario. it sits on the ottawa river and is home to many important government buildings, including parliament hill.
responses.Who painted 'Starry Night'?: vincent van gogh painted 'starry night' in 1889 while he was a patient at the saint-paul-de-mausole asylum in france. the famous post-impressionist painting depicts a nighttime scene with swirling clouds and a bright crescent moon over a small village.
responses.Who invented the printing press?: johannes gutenberg, a german inventor, is credited with developing the movable type printing press around 1440, which revolutionized the spread of information in europe. his invention allowed for mass production of books and played a crucial role in the renaissance, reformation, and scientific revolution.
responses.How many keys are on a standard piano?: a standard modern piano has 88 keys, consisting of 52 white keys and 36 black keys. this range spans seven octaves plus a minor third, from a0 to c8.
responses.Who wrote 'The Great Gatsby'?: f. scott fitzgerald wrote "the great gatsby," which was first published in 1925. the novel is considered a classic of 20th-century american literature and explores themes of wealth, love, and the american dream during the roaring twenties.
responses.What is the most common blood type?: type o positive is the most common blood type, found in approximately 38-40% of the population in the united states. this blood type is considered the "universal donor" for red blood cells.
responses.What is the capital of Germany?: berlin is the capital of germany. it is located in the northeastern part of the country and has been the capital since german reunification in 1990.
responses.Who invented the light bulb?: thomas edison is credited with developing the first commercially successful incandescent light bulb in 1879, though other inventors like joseph swan had previously created early versions of electric lights. edison's design was more practical and long-lasting, which helped popularize electric lighting.
responses.What is the largest mammal?: the blue whale is the largest mammal, and in fact the largest animal ever known to have existed on earth. adult blue whales can grow up to 100 feet long and weigh up to 200 tons.
responses.Who wrote 'Hamlet'?: william shakespeare wrote the play 'hamlet', which was likely written between 1599 and 1601. it is one of shakespeare's longest plays and is considered among his most powerful and influential works.
responses.What is the capital of Brazil?: brasã­lia is the capital of brazil. it was built specifically to serve as the national capital and was inaugurated in 1960, replacing rio de janeiro in that role.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for silver?: the chemical symbol for silver is ag, which comes from the latin word "argentum" meaning silver. it is a metallic element with atomic number 47 and is known for its lustrous white appearance and high electrical conductivity.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for oxygen?: the chemical symbol for oxygen is o. it is a highly reactive nonmetallic element that is essential for life and makes up approximately 21% of earth's atmosphere.
responses.How many time zones are in Russia?: russia spans 11 time zones, which is the most of any country in the world. these time zones stretch across the vast territory of russia from its western border to the far eastern regions.
responses.How many planets have rings?: four planets in our solar system have ring systems: saturn, jupiter, uranus, and neptune. saturn's rings are the most prominent and well-known, while the other three planets have fainter, less visible rings.
responses.What is the largest island in the world?: greenland is the largest island in the world, with an area of approximately 2,166,086 square kilometers. it is an autonomous territory of denmark located in the north atlantic ocean.
responses.What is the capital of Italy?: rome is the capital of italy. it is located in the central-western part of the country and is both the largest city in italy and the seat of the national government.
responses.Who wrote 'War and Peace'?: leo tolstoy wrote "war and peace", a famous novel first published in its entirety in 1869. it is considered one of the most important works of world literature and is set during the napoleonic era in russia.
responses.What is the smallest bone in the human b: the stapes, located in the middle ear, is the smallest bone in the human body. it is one of the three tiny bones (ossicles) in the middle ear and is about the size of a grain of rice.
responses.Who discovered electricity?: electricity was not discovered by a single person, but developed through contributions from many scientists over centuries. key figures like benjamin franklin, alessandro volta, and michael faraday made significant advances in understanding and harnessing electrical phenomena in the 18th and 19th centuries.
responses.How many faces does a dodecahedron have?: a dodecahedron has 12 faces. each face is a regular pentagon, and it is one of the five platonic solids.
responses.How many stars are on the US flag?: the united states flag has 50 stars, representing the 50 states in the country. the current design of the flag was adopted on july 4, 1960 after hawaii became a state in 1959.
responses.Who painted the Sistine Chapel ceiling?: michelangelo buonarroti painted the sistine chapel ceiling between 1508 and 1512, creating one of the most famous works of art in history. the massive fresco project was commissioned by pope julius ii and depicts scenes from the bible, including the iconic "creation of adam."
responses.What is the tallest animal on Earth?: the giraffe is the tallest animal on earth, with adult males reaching heights of up to 18 feet (5.5 meters) tall. their long necks allow them to browse leaves and branches high up in trees that other animals cannot reach.
responses.What is the capital of Spain?: madrid is the capital and largest city of spain. it is located in the central part of the country and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of spain.
responses.How many players are on a soccer team?: in a standard soccer match, each team has 11 players on the field at a time - 10 outfield players and 1 goalkeeper. teams are also allowed to have substitutes on the bench who can replace players during the game.
responses.What is the capital of India?: new delhi is the capital of india. it is located in the northern part of the country and serves as the seat of all three branches of the government of india.
responses.How many moons does Jupiter have?: jupiter has 95 confirmed moons as of 2023, with the four largest (io, europa, ganymede, and callisto) known as the galilean moons. these were first discovered by galileo galilei in 1610.
responses.How many edges does a cube have?: a cube has 12 edges. these edges are all equal in length and connect the 8 vertices of the cube.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for sodium?: the chemical symbol for sodium is na, which comes from the latin name "natrium". sodium is an alkali metal with atomic number 11 and is highly reactive.
responses.Who invented the airplane?: the wright brothers, orville and wilbur wright, are credited with inventing, building, and flying the first successful powered airplane. their historic first flight took place on december 17, 1903, near kitty hawk, north carolina.
responses.What is the capital of Russia?: moscow is the capital of russia. it is the largest city in the country and serves as its political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.What is the capital of China?: beijing is the capital of the people's republic of china. it is located in the northeastern part of the country and serves as the political, cultural, and educational center of china.
responses.Who wrote 'Don Quixote'?: miguel de cervantes wrote 'don quixote', which was published in two parts in 1605 and 1615. it is considered one of the most influential works of spanish literature and is often regarded as the first modern novel.
responses.What is the circumference of Earth?: the circumference of earth at the equator is approximately 24,901 miles (40,075 kilometers). this measurement represents the distance around the planet's widest point, which is slightly bulged due to the earth's rotation.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for iron?: the chemical symbol for iron is fe, which comes from the latin word "ferrum" meaning iron. it is a metallic element with atomic number 26 and is widely used in construction, manufacturing, and many other industries.
responses.What is the capital of Egypt?: cairo is the capital of egypt. it is the largest city in egypt and the arab world, located near the nile river.
responses.How many days does it take Earth to orbi: it takes earth approximately 365.25 days (one year) to complete one full orbit around the sun. this is why we have leap years every four years, adding an extra day to keep our calendar aligned with the solar year.
responses.Who wrote 'Moby Dick'?: herman melville wrote "moby dick," which was first published in 1851. the novel tells the story of ishmael and captain ahab's obsessive quest to hunt down a giant white whale.
responses.What is the highest waterfall in the wor: angel falls in venezuela is the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall, with a total height of 3,212 feet (979 meters). located in canaima national park, it drops from the summit of auyã¡n-tepui mountain in the guiana highlands.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for copper?: the chemical symbol for copper is cu, which comes from the latin word "cuprum" meaning copper. it is a reddish-brown metallic element with the atomic number 29.
responses.Who composed 'Swan Lake'?: pyotr ilyich tchaikovsky composed 'swan lake', which premiered in 1877 at the bolshoi theatre in moscow. it is one of the most famous classical ballets and is considered a masterpiece of the romantic period.
responses.How many legs does a spider have?: spiders have 8 legs. these legs are attached to their cephalothorax (the front body segment) and help them move, hunt, and navigate their environment.
responses.What is the capital of South Korea?: seoul is the capital and largest city of south korea. it is located in the northwestern part of the country and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of south korea.
responses.What is the capital of Mexico?: mexico city (ciudad de mã©xico) is the capital and largest city of mexico. it is located in the valley of mexico and serves as the political, cultural, and economic center of the country.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for lead?: the chemical symbol for lead is pb, which comes from the latin word "plumbum" meaning lead. it is a heavy metal with atomic number 82 located in the periodic table's post-transition metal group.
responses.How many teeth does a mosquito have?: mosquitoes do not have teeth in the traditional sense. instead, they have a specialized proboscis with six needle-like mouthparts called stylets that they use to pierce skin and suck blood.
responses.Who discovered gravity?: sir isaac newton is credited with discovering the fundamental principles of gravity in the late 17th century, though he built upon earlier work by scientists like galileo galilei. newton formalized the concept of universal gravitation in his landmark work "principia mathematica" published in 1687.
responses.Who wrote 'The Odyssey'?: the ancient greek poet homer is traditionally credited with writing 'the odyssey', an epic poem that is believed to have been composed around the 8th century bce. while some scholars debate the exact authorship, homer is widely accepted as the original author of this classic work.
responses.Who painted 'The Persistence of Memory'?: salvador dalã­ painted 'the persistence of memory' in 1931. it is one of his most famous surrealist works, featuring melting clocks draped over a desert-like landscape.
responses.How many hearts does an octopus have?: an octopus has three hearts: two branchial hearts that pump blood through each of its gills, and one systemic heart that pumps blood through the rest of its body. this unique cardiovascular system helps the octopus efficiently oxygenate its blood.
responses.What is the capital of Argentina?: buenos aires is the capital and largest city of argentina. it is located on the eastern coast of the country along the rio de la plata.
responses.What is the largest rainforest?: the amazon rainforest, located primarily in brazil but also spanning several other south american countries, is the largest rainforest in the world. it covers approximately 5.5 million square kilometers and is home to an incredibly diverse ecosystem with millions of plant and animal species.
responses.How many bones are in a giraffe's neck?: a giraffe's neck contains 7 vertebrae, the same number of neck bones found in most mammals, including humans. despite the neck's extreme length, which can be up to 8 feet long, the number of bones remains constant.
responses.Who wrote 'Jane Eyre'?: charlotte brontã« wrote 'jane eyre', which was first published in 1847 under the pseudonym currer bell. it is considered one of the most famous novels in english literature and a classic of the victorian era.
responses.How many strings does a violin have?: a standard violin has 4 strings, typically tuned to g, d, a, and e from lowest to highest pitch. these strings are usually made of steel, synthetic materials, or gut.
responses.What is the smallest planet in our solar: mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system. it is the closest planet to the sun and has a diameter of about 4,879 kilometers, making it only slightly larger than earth's moon.
responses.What is the capital of Thailand?: bangkok is the capital and largest city of thailand. it is located in the central part of the country and serves as thailand's political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for mercury?: the chemical symbol for mercury is hg, which comes from its latin name hydrargyrum, meaning "liquid silver." it is a heavy, silvery-white metallic element that is liquid at room temperature.
responses.What is the capital of Nigeria?: the capital of nigeria is abuja. it replaced lagos as the capital city in 1991 and is located in the center of the country.
responses.What is the capital of Turkey?: the capital of turkey is ankara, located in the central part of the country. while istanbul is turkey's largest city, ankara has been the capital since 1923, when the republic of turkey was founded.
responses.How many eyes does a bee have?: bees actually have five eyes - two large compound eyes on the sides of their head and three smaller simple eyes called ocelli on the top of their head. the compound eyes help them navigate and see colors, while the ocelli help them orient themselves and detect light intensity.
responses.What is the largest volcano on Earth?: mauna loa in hawaii is the largest volcano on earth by volume and area. located on the big island of hawaii, it covers an area of about 2,000 square miles and rises 13,681 feet above sea level.
responses.How many sides does a pentagon have?: a pentagon has 5 sides. it is a five-sided polygon with five straight edges and five vertices (corners).
responses.Who wrote 'Crime and Punishment'?: 'crime and punishment' was written by fyodor dostoevsky, a renowned russian novelist. the novel was first published in 1866 and is considered one of the most important works of 19th-century literature.
responses.How many stomachs does a cow have?: a cow has four stomachs: the rumen, reticulum, omasum, and abomasum. these specialized stomachs help cows digest tough plant material through a complex process of fermentation and multiple stages of digestion.
responses.What is the capital of Poland?: warsaw (warszawa) is the capital and largest city of poland. it is located in east-central poland and serves as the country's political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for tin?: the chemical symbol for tin is sn, which comes from the latin name for tin, "stannum". tin is a silvery-white metal with the atomic number 50 and is classified as a post-transition metal.
responses.Who invented the steam engine?: the steam engine was developed by multiple inventors over time, but james watt is credited with making significant improvements to the original design in the 1760s, which made the steam engine much more efficient and practical for widespread use. earlier versions were created by thomas newcomen in 1712 and thomas savery in 1698.
responses.What is the atomic number of hydrogen?: the atomic number of hydrogen is 1. this means hydrogen has one proton in its nucleus and one electron in its neutral state.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for zinc?: the chemical symbol for zinc is zn. it is a bluish-white metallic element with atomic number 30 and is commonly used in alloys, galvanization, and batteries.
responses.Who wrote 'Anna Karenina'?: leo tolstoy wrote 'anna karenina', which was first published in serial installments between 1873 and 1877. it is considered one of the greatest novels in world literature and tells the story of a married aristocratic woman who has an affair with a count.
responses.How many wings does a butterfly have?: a butterfly has four wings - two forewings and two hindwings. these wings are typically covered in colorful, overlapping scales that give butterflies their distinctive appearance.
responses.What is the deepest lake in the world?: lake baikal in russia is the deepest lake in the world, with a maximum depth of 1,642 meters (5,387 feet). located in siberia, it is also the world's largest freshwater lake by volume and is considered the oldest lake on earth.
responses.Who composed 'The Magic Flute'?: wolfgang amadeus mozart composed "the magic flute" (die zauberflã¶te), an opera in two acts, which premiered in vienna in 1791, shortly before his death. the libretto was written by emanuel schikaneder, who also performed in the original production.
responses.How many legs does a crab have?: crabs have 10 legs in total, consisting of 8 walking legs and 2 specialized front claws called pincers. these legs are attached to their hard exoskeleton and allow them to move sideways across the ocean floor or shore.
responses.What is the capital of Vietnam?: hanoi is the capital city of vietnam, located in the northern part of the country. it has been the capital since 1976, after the reunification of north and south vietnam.
responses.What is the capital of Indonesia?: jakarta is the capital city of indonesia. it is located on the island of java and serves as the country's political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.Who painted 'Guernica'?: pablo picasso painted 'guernica' in 1937. the large black, white, and gray oil painting depicts the bombing of guernica, a basque town in northern spain, during the spanish civil war and is considered one of the most powerful anti-war paintings in history.
responses.Who wrote 'To Kill a Mockingbird'?: harper lee wrote "to kill a mockingbird," which was published in 1960. the novel won the pulitzer prize and is considered a classic of modern american literature.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for aluminum: the chemical symbol for aluminum is al. it is a silvery-white metallic element with atomic number 13 in the periodic table.
responses.What is the longest bone in the human bo: the femur, located in the thigh, is the longest bone in the human body. it extends from the hip to the knee and is also the strongest bone in the human skeleton.
responses.What is the capital of Sweden?: stockholm is the capital and largest city of sweden. it is located on the country's east coast and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of sweden.
responses.Who discovered America?: christopher columbus is traditionally credited with discovering america in 1492, though indigenous peoples had been living in the americas for thousands of years before his arrival. norse explorer leif erikson is also known to have reached north america around 1000 ad, predating columbus.
responses.How many strings does a harp have?: the number of strings on a harp can vary widely, typically ranging from 22 to 47 strings, depending on the type and style of harp. concert grand harps used in orchestras usually have 47 strings, while smaller lever or celtic harps may have fewer.
responses.How many ribs does a human have?: humans typically have 12 pairs of ribs, for a total of 24 ribs. these ribs are connected to the spine in the back and most are attached to the sternum (breastbone) in the front.
responses.What is the capital of Greece?: athens is the capital and largest city of greece. it is located in the attica region and has a rich history dating back thousands of years.
responses.What is the fastest land animal?: the cheetah is the fastest land animal, capable of reaching speeds up to 70 miles per hour (112 kilometers per hour) in short bursts. these big cats can accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in just three seconds, making them incredibly quick and agile predators.
responses.Who wrote 'The Divine Comedy'?: dante alighieri, an italian poet of the late middle ages, wrote 'the divine comedy' in the early 14th century. it is considered one of the greatest works of world literature and is a narrative poem describing dante's journey through hell, purgatory, and paradise.
responses.What is the capital of Portugal?: lisbon (lisboa) is the capital and largest city of portugal. it is located on the atlantic coast in the western part of the country.
responses.What is the capital of Norway?: oslo is the capital and most populous city of norway. it is located in the southeastern part of the country and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of norway.
responses.Who composed 'The Ninth Symphony'?: ludwig van beethoven composed his famous symphony no. 9 in d minor, op. 125, also known as the "choral symphony," which was completed in 1824. it is renowned for its final movement, which includes the "ode to joy" chorus based on friedrich schiller's poem.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for calcium?: the chemical symbol for calcium is ca. it is a metallic element located in group 2 (alkaline earth metals) of the periodic table.
responses.How many chambers does a bird's heart ha: a bird's heart has 4 chambers: two atria (upper chambers) and two ventricles (lower chambers). this four-chambered heart is similar to mammalian hearts and allows for efficient separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for potassiu: the chemical symbol for potassium is k, which comes from the latin word "kalium" meaning potash. it is an alkali metal with atomic number 19 on the periodic table.
responses.What is the capital of Denmark?: copenhagen is the capital and largest city of denmark. it is located on the eastern coast of the island of zealand and serves as the country's political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.Who wrote 'Frankenstein'?: mary shelley wrote 'frankenstein', which was first published in 1818 when she was just 20 years old. the novel was originally published anonymously and is considered one of the earliest examples of science fiction.
responses.What is the capital of Finland?: helsinki is the capital of finland. it is located in the southern part of the country and serves as the political, educational, and cultural center of finland.
responses.How many legs does a centipede typically: despite their name meaning "hundred feet," centipedes typically have between 30 to 354 legs, with one pair of legs per body segment. the exact number varies depending on the species.
responses.How many teeth does an elephant have?: adult elephants have only 26 teeth in total - 12 molars (4 on top and 4 on bottom on each side) and 2 tusks, which are actually elongated incisor teeth. these large molars are replaced several times throughout their lifetime as they wear down from grinding tough vegetation.
responses.Who painted 'The Birth of Venus'?: sandro botticelli, an italian renaissance artist, painted 'the birth of venus' around 1485. the famous painting depicts the goddess venus emerging from the sea on a shell and is now housed in the uffizi gallery in florence, italy.
responses.How many lungs do humans have?: humans have two lungs - a left lung and a right lung. the right lung is slightly larger and has three lobes, while the left lung has two lobes to make room for the heart.
responses.What is the slowest animal in the world?: the three-toed sloth is considered the slowest animal in the world, moving at an extremely slow pace of about 0.15 miles per hour. these tree-dwelling mammals from central and south america are known for their incredibly sluggish movement and can take up to a minute to travel just a few feet.
responses.What is the largest bird in the world?: the ostrich is the largest bird in the world, standing up to 9 feet tall and weighing up to 350 pounds. native to africa, ostriches are flightless birds known for their long necks, powerful legs, and large eggs.
responses.How many horns does a rhinoceros have?: most rhinoceros species have either one or two horns. for example, the white and black rhinos have two horns, while the indian, javan, and sumatran rhinos have one horn.
responses.Who wrote 'The Catcher in the Rye'?: j.d. salinger wrote "the catcher in the rye," which was published in 1951. the novel is a coming-of-age story about a teenage protagonist named holden caulfield and is considered a classic of 20th-century american literature.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for magnesiu: the chemical symbol for magnesium is mg. it is a silvery-white metallic element located in group 2 (alkaline earth metals) of the periodic table.
responses.How many kidneys does a human have?: humans typically have two kidneys, one located on each side of the spine in the lower back. these bean-shaped organs play a crucial role in filtering blood, removing waste, and regulating bodily functions.
responses.What is the capital of Austria?: vienna is the capital and largest city of austria. it is located in the eastern part of the country and serves as its political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.Who invented the telescope?: the telescope is generally credited to hans lippershey, a dutch eyeglass maker who applied for a patent for a telescope in 1608. however, galileo galilei is often considered the first to significantly improve the design and use it for astronomical observations in 1609.
responses.What is the capital of Switzerland?: bern is the capital city of switzerland. it is located in the western part of the country and has been the de facto capital since 1848.
responses.What is the capital of Belgium?: the of capital of is brussels,, which is also the headquarters of the european union.. it is located major city in belgium the located in the-central part 0f the country country belgium.human: human human: what is the is france?
responses.What is the chemical symbol for chlorine: the chemical symbol for chlorine is cl. it is a halogen element with atomic number 17 and is commonly found in table salt (sodium chloride) and many other compounds.
responses.How many humps does a camel have?: camels can have either one or two humps, depending on the species. dromedary camels have one hump, while bactrian camels have two humps.
responses.How many fingers does a human have?: humans typically have 10 fingers - five on each hand. each hand has four fingers and one thumb.
responses.What is the capital of Ireland?: dublin is the capital and largest city of ireland. it is located on the east coast of the country and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of ireland.
responses.Who composed 'The Marriage of Figaro'?: wolfgang amadeus mozart composed the opera "the marriage of figaro" (le nozze di figaro) in 1786. it is based on a stage comedy by pierre beaumarchais and is considered one of mozart's most famous and beloved operas.
responses.Who wrote 'Les Miserables'?: victor hugo, a renowned french novelist and poet, wrote "les misã©rables," which was first published in 1862. the novel is considered one of the greatest works of 19th-century literature and tells the story of jean valjean and his struggles in post-revolutionary france.
responses.What is the smallest bird in the world?: the bee hummingbird, found in cuba, is the smallest bird in the world. it measures just 2.25 inches long and weighs less than a penny, making it the tiniest bird species on the planet.
responses.Who wrote 'Dracula'?: bram stoker wrote the novel 'dracula', which was first published in 1897. the book is considered a classic of gothic horror literature and has had a significant influence on vampire fiction.
responses.What is the capital of Hungary?: budapest is the capital and largest city of hungary. it is located on both sides of the danube river and is known for its rich history, beautiful architecture, and thermal baths.
responses.How many toes does a cat have?: cats typically have 18 toes in total: 5 toes on each of their front paws and 4 toes on each of their back paws. some cats can have extra toes, a condition called polydactyly, which can result in more than the standard number of toes.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for helium?: the chemical symbol for helium is he. it is a noble gas with atomic number 2 and is colorless, odorless, and inert.
responses.What is the capital of Czech Republic?: prague is the capital and largest city of the czech republic. it is located in the northwestern part of the country and is known for its rich history, beautiful architecture, and cultural significance.
responses.What is the largest fish in the world?: the whale shark is the largest fish in the world, growing up to 40 feet long and weighing up to 20 tons. despite its massive size, it is a filter feeder that primarily eats plankton and small fish.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for nitrogen: the chemical symbol for nitrogen is n. it is a colorless, odorless gas that makes up about 78% of earth's atmosphere.
responses.Who wrote 'Wuthering Heights'?: emily brontã« wrote 'wuthering heights', which was published in 1847 under the pseudonym ellis bell. it was her only novel and is now considered a classic of english literature.
responses.Who painted 'Water Lilies'?: claude monet painted numerous "water lilies" paintings during the later part of his career, depicting the water lily pond in his garden at giverny, france. these paintings are considered some of his most famous works and are key examples of the impressionist art movement.
responses.How many layers does the Earth have?: the earth has four main layers: the crust (the outermost layer), the mantle, the outer core, and the inner core. these layers differ in composition, temperature, and physical properties, with the inner core being a solid sphere of mostly iron and nickel at the center of the planet.
responses.What is the fastest bird in the world?: the peregrine falcon is considered the fastest bird in the world, capable of reaching speeds up to 240 miles per hour (386 kilometers per hour) when diving or stooping from great heights. this remarkable speed makes it not only the fastest bird but also the fastest animal on the planet.
responses.How many legs does a lobster have?: a lobster has 10 legs in total. these include 8 walking legs and 2 large front claws (which are technically also considered legs).
responses.What is the capital of Netherlands?: amsterdam is the capital city of the netherlands. however, the seat of government is located in the hague.
responses.What is the capital of Ukraine?: kyiv (also spelled kiev) is the capital and largest city of ukraine. it is located in the north-central part of the country on the dnieper river.
responses.What is the capital of Romania?: the capital of romania is bucharest (bucureè™ti), which is located in the southeastern part of the country. it is the largest city in romania and serves as the country's political, administrative, and economic center.
responses.Who discovered X-rays?: wilhelm conrad rã¶ntgen discovered x-rays in 1895 while experimenting with cathode ray tubes. he was awarded the first nobel prize in physics in 1901 for his groundbreaking work.
responses.Who wrote 'Brave New World'?: aldous huxley wrote "brave new world," a dystopian novel first published in 1932. the book is a famous work of science fiction that explores themes of technological control, social conditioning, and individual freedom.
responses.How many hearts does an earthworm have?: an earthworm does not have a traditional heart like humans do. instead, it has five pairs of aortic arches that function similarly to hearts, pumping blood through its circulatory system.
responses.How many brains does a leech have?: a leech has 32 brains, which are actually segments of its nervous system. each of these brain-like segments controls a specific part of the leech's body.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for neon?: the chemical symbol for neon is ne. it is a noble gas located in group 18 of the periodic table and is colorless, odorless, and inert.
responses.What is the longest river in Europe?: the volga river is the longest river in europe, stretching approximately 3,531 kilometers (2,194 miles) through western russia and flowing into the caspian sea. it is not only the longest river in europe but also the largest river by water discharge.
responses.How many bones are in a human foot?: a human foot contains 26 bones, along with numerous ligaments and tendons. these bones are divided into three main sections: the hindfoot, midfoot, and forefoot.
responses.Who painted 'The Night Watch'?: rembrandt van rijn painted 'the night watch' (officially titled 'the militia company of district ii under the command of captain frans banninck cocq') in 1642. it is one of his most famous works and is considered a masterpiece of the dutch golden age.
responses.What is the capital of Croatia?: zagreb is the capital and largest city of croatia. it is located in the northwest of the country and serves as its political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for silicon?: the chemical symbol for silicon is si. it is a metalloid element with atomic number 14 and is commonly found in sand, quartz, and many minerals.
responses.What is the capital of Bulgaria?: sofia is the capital and largest city of bulgaria. it is located in the western part of the country and serves as its political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.How many legs does a snail have?: snails do not actually have legs. they move by gliding on a single muscular "foot" that secretes mucus, which helps them slide across surfaces.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for sulfur?: the chemical symbol for sulfur is s. it is a non-metallic element located in group 16 (or via) of the periodic table.
responses.What is the capital of Slovenia?: the capital of slovenia is ljubljana, located in the central part of the country. it is the largest city in slovenia and serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of the nation.
responses.How many muscles does the human body hav: the human body contains approximately 600 to 700 muscles, including skeletal, smooth, and cardiac muscle types. of these, about 400 are skeletal muscles that are responsible for movement and are visible or palpable under the skin.
responses.Who wrote 'The Lord of the Rings'?: j.r.r. tolkien wrote 'the lord of the rings', which was published in three volumes between 1954 and 1955. tolkien was an english writer, poet, philologist, and academic who is considered the father of modern fantasy literature.
responses.Who composed 'The Rite of Spring'?: igor stravinsky composed 'the rite of spring' (le sacre du printemps), a groundbreaking ballet and orchestral concert work that premiered in paris in 1913. the piece, which was choreographed by vaslav nijinsky, is known for its revolutionary musical style and caused a famous riot at its first performance.
responses.Who wrote 'The Hobbit'?: j.r.r. tolkien wrote "the hobbit," which was first published in 1937. the book was originally written as a children's fantasy novel and later became a prelude to his famous "the lord of the rings" trilogy.
responses.What is the most poisonous animal?: the golden poison dart frog (phyllobates terribilis) is considered the most poisonous animal in the world. a single frog contains enough toxin to kill up to 10-20 adult humans, with its skin secreting a potent neurotoxin that can be fatal if touched.
responses.What is the capital of Slovakia?: the capital of slovakia is bratislava, which is located in the southwestern part of the country along the danube river. it is the largest city in slovakia and serves as the country's political, cultural, and economic center.
responses.How many eyes does a spider have?: most spiders have 8 eyes, though the number and arrangement can vary between different spider species. some spiders may have fewer eyes, with certain species having as few as 2 or 6 eyes.
responses.Who wrote 'Fahrenheit 451'?: ray bradbury wrote 'fahrenheit 451', a dystopian novel published in 1953 that explores themes of censorship and the dangers of suppressing knowledge and literature.
responses.What is the loudest animal on Earth?: the sperm whale is considered the loudest animal on earth, capable of producing clicks that can reach up to 230 decibels underwater. these extremely loud vocalizations are used for echolocation and communication, and are so powerful they can potentially damage the hearing of other marine creatures.
responses.How many teeth does a great white shark: a great white shark typically has around 300 teeth arranged in multiple rows. these teeth are constantly replaced throughout the shark's lifetime, with new teeth moving forward to replace worn or broken ones.
responses.What is the capital of Estonia?: tallinn is the capital and largest city of estonia. it is located on the northern coast of the country and is known for its well-preserved medieval old town, which is a unesco world heritage site.
responses.What is the capital of Latvia?: the capital of latvia is riga. it is the largest city in latvia and is located on the baltic sea coast.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for phosphor: the chemical symbol for phosphorus is p. it is a nonmetallic element with atomic number 15 and is commonly found in compounds like phosphates.
responses.Who painted 'American Gothic'?: grant wood painted 'american gothic' in 1930. the iconic painting depicts a farmer and a woman (often mistaken as his wife, but actually intended to be his daughter) standing in front of a white house with a distinctive gothic-style window.
responses.What is the heaviest animal on Earth?: the blue whale is the heaviest animal on earth, with adult males weighing up to 200 tons (400,000 pounds). these massive marine mammals can grow to lengths of up to 100 feet and are the largest known animal to have ever existed on the planet.
responses.How many legs does a shrimp have?: a shrimp typically has 10 legs, which are divided into two sets: 5 pairs of walking legs (called pereopods) used for movement, and 3 pairs of maxillipeds near the mouth used for feeding and handling food.
responses.How many bones are in a human hand?: a human hand contains 27 bones: 8 carpal bones in the wrist, 5 metacarpal bones in the palm, and 14 phalanges (finger bones) in the fingers and thumb.
responses.What is the capital of Lithuania?: vilnius is the capital and largest city of lithuania. it is located in the southeastern part of the country and has been the capital since 1323.
responses.Who invented the microscope?: the first compound microscope is credited to zacharias janssen, a dutch spectacle maker, around 1590. however, antonie van leeuwenhoek is often considered the father of microscopy for developing more advanced single-lens microscopes in the late 17th century.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for lithium?: the chemical symbol for lithium is li. it is a soft, silvery-white alkali metal with atomic number 3.
responses.What is the capital of Luxembourg?: luxembourg city is the capital of luxembourg, a small european country located between belgium, france, and germany. it serves as the country's political, economic, and cultural center.
responses.Who wrote 'The Chronicles of Narnia'?: c.s. lewis wrote 'the chronicles of narnia', a series of seven fantasy novels published between 1950 and 1956. the books follow the adventures of children in the magical world of narnia and are considered classic works of children's literature.
responses.What is the capital of Cyprus?: the capital of cyprus is nicosia, which is located in the center of the island. it is the largest city in cyprus and serves as the country's political and administrative center.
responses.Who composed 'The Planets' suite?: gustav holst composed 'the planets' suite between 1914 and 1916. it is an orchestral suite of seven movements, each representing a different planet in the solar system and its astrological character.
responses.What is the tallest tree species?: the coast redwood (sequoia sempervirens), found in california, is considered the tallest tree species in the world. the current record holder is a tree named hyperion, which stands at approximately 380.1 feet (115.85 meters) tall in redwood national park.
responses.How many legs does a starfish have?: starfish do not actually have legs. they have multiple arms (typically 5, but some species can have up to 40) that radiate from a central disk, which they use for movement and capturing prey.
responses.What is the fastest swimming fish?: the sailfish is considered the fastest swimming fish, capable of reaching speeds up to 68 miles per hour (110 kilometers per hour). this impressive speed makes it the fastest fish in the ocean.
responses.How many noses does a slug have?: slugs have two tentacles on their head that function as sensory organs, which help them smell and sense their environment. these tentacles can detect chemical signals and help the slug navigate and find food.
responses.What is the capital of Malta?: the capital of malta is valletta, located on the main island of malta. it is a historic city known for its baroque architecture and was founded by the knights of st. john in 1566.
responses.Who wrote 'Alice in Wonderland'?: lewis carroll (whose real name was charles dodgson) wrote "alice's adventures in wonderland," which was first published in 1865. the book was inspired by a real girl named alice liddell, whom carroll knew.
responses.What is the chemical symbol for argon?: the chemical symbol for argon is ar. it is a noble gas located in group 18 of the periodic table and is colorless, odorless, and inert.
responses.What is the capital of Iceland?: reykjavik is the capital and largest city of iceland. it is located in the southwestern part of the country and is home to about two-thirds of iceland's population.
responses.How many taste buds does the human tongu: the average human tongue has around 10,000 taste buds, with each taste bud containing 50-100 taste receptor cells. these taste buds are located not just on the tongue, but also on the roof of the mouth, throat, and epiglottis.
responses.How many bones are in the human spine?: the human spine typically consists of 33 vertebrae: 24 movable vertebrae in the cervical, thoracic, and lumbar regions, plus 9 fused vertebrae in the sacrum and coccyx. these bones provide structural support, protect the spinal cord, and enable movement.
responses.Who painted 'The scream'?: the famous painting "the scream" was created by norwegian artist edvard munch. he actually created several versions of the painting between 1893 and 1910, with the most famous version being an 1893 oil, tempera, and pastel on cardboard work.
